methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $800M - $7.6B.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $6.86B

data.narrative:
Archer Aviation, a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, carries a current market valuation of $3.33B despite raising $1.5B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of four same-sector peers spanning similar valuation tiers: Zipline ($7.6B, Series G), Joby Aviation ($6.9B, public), Nuro ($6.0B, Series G), and Elroy Air ($800M, Series B). This peer set, while limited to private and recently public participants, reflects the nascent maturity of the autonomous vehicle ecosystem.

The peer-median method, the sole applicable triangulation approach given data constraints, yields an implied valuation of $6.4B. This median reflects the compressed range across the comparable set, with a floor of $800M and ceiling of $7.6B. The dispersion—spanning nearly one order of magnitude—underscores stage-dependent valuation volatility in the sector; Series B and Series G companies command multiples reflecting different risk profiles and technical de-risking trajectories.

The triangulated range supports a valuation band of $5.5B to $7.4B, centered at $6.4B—approximately 92% above Archer's current market price. However, key uncertainties remain material: the peer set lacks sufficient depth to control for differentiation in technology maturity, regulatory pathway clarity, or path-to-revenue timing. Additional methodologies incorporating revenue multiples or DCF analysis would be required to validate this peer-derived indication.
